text:
**IBEW Traveler Snapshot — Monday, July 6, 2026**

**LU-481 Indianapolis, IN** — The Meta data center play is still firing. Meade Electric's pulling **$53.80/hr + 10% rollups** on 5-12s Monday-Friday with Saturday work; Industrial Electric's running Lilly LP1 on rotating 7-10s with that 13-day rule. Book's at 187 hands against 43 calls—move fast if you're chasing Lebanon.

**LU-494 Milwaukee, WI** — Port Washington data center repost is the move. Four contractors (MJ, Gurtz, Cupertino, Uihlein) are all running the same deal: **JW wage + $10/hr base**, ASAP starts. 41 calls, 169 hands—thinnest ratio here means faster callbacks. This one's reprinting because they need bodies.

**LU-379 Charlotte, NC** — Three-state data center circuit with real money. Rosendin's MDN.06 in Newton/Maiden hits **$3.50/hr over scale** (37.37 base) on night shift, **$150 boot voucher after 90**, and 48-54 hours per week. Gross Electric and Miller Electric both holding standing calls at QTS and MOC-1. Top scale **$37.37**.

**LU-1426 Grand Forks, ND** — Polaris Forge (three Parsons Electric calls: 33, 28, 14 heads) pays **$44.80/hr + $100/day non-taxed per diem** Monday-Friday, bumps to **$150 Saturday-Sunday**. Shift differential on nights hits **31.4%**. 122 hands, 25 calls—rural play with real incentive muscle.

**LU-683 Columbus, OH** — Mid City and TSG are solid at **$73.42/hr** on various work. ESI's Cologix data center runs 5-10s + 8 with **all OT at double time**. Tightest board here: 23 calls, 117 hands. Book's moving.

**LU-26 Lanham, MD** — Government and hospital work: Aarow Electric on INOVA Springfield new construction plus DOE DC tenant work. Citizen/Real ID/OSHA 10 required. 37 calls, 99 hands, but work's scattered across multiple locations.

**The trend is unmistakable: data center builds are pulling hands north and west—Midwest and Southeast are the hottest—while traditional commercial and heavy industrial work tightens the boards everywhere else.**
